markers.arabic_text: بِسْمِ ٱللَّهِ ٱلرَّحْمَٰنِ ٱلرَّحِيمِ إِنَّآ أَرْسَلْنَا نُوحًا إِلَىٰ قَوْمِهِۦٓ أَنْ أَنذِرْ قَوْمَكَ مِن قَبْلِ أَن يَأْتِيَهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌۭ
markers.english_text: BEHOLD. We sent Noah unto his people. [saying:] "Warn thy people ere grievous suffering befall them!

markers.arabic_text: قَالَ يَٰقَوْمِ إِنِّى لَكُمْ نَذِيرٌۭ مُّبِينٌ
markers.english_text: [And Noah] said: "O my people! I am but a plain warner to you, [sent to tell you]
that you should worship God [alone] and be conscious of Him. "Now do pay heed unto me,

markers.arabic_text: يَغْفِرْ لَكُم مِّن ذُنُوبِكُمْ وَيُؤَخِّرْكُمْ إِلَىٰٓ أَجَلٍۢ مُّسَمًّى ۚ إِنَّ أَجَلَ ٱللَّهِ إِذَا جَآءَ لَا يُؤَخَّرُ ۖ لَوْ كُنتُمْ تَعْلَمُونَ
markers.english_text: so that He may forgive you some of your sins, and grant you respite until a term known [to Him alone]: but, behold, when the term appointed by God does come, it can never be put back - if you but knew it!"

markers.arabic_text: قَالَ رَبِّ إِنِّى دَعَوْتُ قَوْمِى لَيْلًۭا وَنَهَارًۭا
markers.english_text: [And after a time, Noah] said: "O my Sustainer! Verily, I have been calling unto my people night and day
but my call has only caused them to flee farther and farther away [from Thee].

markers.arabic_text: وَإِنِّى كُلَّمَا دَعَوْتُهُمْ لِتَغْفِرَ لَهُمْ جَعَلُوٓا۟ أَصَٰبِعَهُمْ فِىٓ ءَاذَانِهِمْ وَٱسْتَغْشَوْا۟ ثِيَابَهُمْ وَأَصَرُّوا۟ وَٱسْتَكْبَرُوا۟ ٱسْتِكْبَارًۭا
markers.english_text: And behold, whenever I called unto them with a view to Thy granting them forgiveness, they put their fingers into their ears, and wrapped themselves up in their garments [of sin]; and grew obstinate, and became [yet more] arrogant in their false pride.
"And behold I called unto them openly;

markers.arabic_text: ثُمَّ إِنِّىٓ أَعْلَنتُ لَهُمْ وَأَسْرَرْتُ لَهُمْ إِسْرَارًۭا
markers.english_text: and, behold I preached to them in public; and I spoke to them secretly, in private;

markers.arabic_text: فَقُلْتُ ٱسْتَغْفِرُوا۟ رَبَّكُمْ إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ غَفَّارًۭا
markers.english_text: and I said: "'Ask your Sustainer to forgive you your sins - for, verily, He is all-forgiving!
He will shower upon you heavenly blessings abundant,

markers.arabic_text: وَيُمْدِدْكُم بِأَمْوَٰلٍۢ وَبَنِينَ وَيَجْعَل لَّكُمْ جَنَّٰتٍۢ وَيَجْعَل لَّكُمْ أَنْهَٰرًۭا
markers.english_text: and will aid you with worldly goods and children, and will bestow upon you gardens, and bestow upon you running waters.

markers.arabic_text: مَّا لَكُمْ لَا تَرْجُونَ لِلَّهِ وَقَارًۭا
markers.english_text: "'What is amiss with you that you cannot look forward to God's majesty,
seeing that He has created [every one of] you in successive stages?

markers.arabic_text: أَلَمْ تَرَوْا۟ كَيْفَ خَلَقَ ٱللَّهُ سَبْعَ سَمَٰوَٰتٍۢ طِبَاقًۭا
markers.english_text: "'Do you not see how God has created seven heavens in full harmony with one another,

markers.arabic_text: وَجَعَلَ ٱلْقَمَرَ فِيهِنَّ نُورًۭا وَجَعَلَ ٱلشَّمْسَ سِرَاجًۭا
markers.english_text: and has set up within them the moon as a light [reflected] and set up the sun as a [radiant] lamp?
"'And God has caused you to grow out of the earth in [gradual] growth; and thereafter He will return you to it [in death]:
"'And God has made the earth a wide expanse for you,
so that you might walk thereon on spacious paths.’"

markers.arabic_text: قَالَ نُوحٌۭ رَّبِّ إِنَّهُمْ عَصَوْنِى وَٱتَّبَعُوا۟ مَن لَّمْ يَزِدْهُ مَالُهُۥ وَوَلَدُهُۥٓ إِلَّا خَسَارًۭا
markers.english_text: [And] Noah continued: "O my Sustainer! Behold, they have opposed me [throughout], for they follow people whose wealth and children lead them increasingly into ruin,
and who have devised a most awesome blasphemy [against Thee],

markers.arabic_text: وَقَالُوا۟ لَا تَذَرُنَّ ءَالِهَتَكُمْ وَلَا تَذَرُنَّ وَدًّۭا وَلَا سُوَاعًۭا وَلَا يَغُوثَ وَيَعُوقَ وَنَسْرًۭا
markers.english_text: inasmuch as they said [to their followers], 'Do not ever abandon your gods: abandon neither Wadd nor Suwa', and neither Yaghuth nor Ya’uq nor Nasr!'

markers.arabic_text: وَقَدْ أَضَلُّوا۟ كَثِيرًۭا ۖ وَلَا تَزِدِ ٱلظَّٰلِمِينَ إِلَّا ضَلَٰلًۭا
markers.english_text: "And so they have led many a one astray: hence, ordain Thou that these evildoers stray but farther and farther away [from all that they may desire]!"

markers.arabic_text: مِّمَّا خَطِيٓـَٰٔتِهِمْ أُغْرِقُوا۟ فَأُدْخِلُوا۟ نَارًۭا فَلَمْ يَجِدُوا۟ لَهُم مِّن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ أَنصَارًۭا
markers.english_text: And so, because of their sins, they were drowned [in the great flood], and were doomed to suffer the fire [of the hereafter]; and they found none who could succour them against God.

markers.arabic_text: وَقَالَ نُوحٌۭ رَّبِّ لَا تَذَرْ عَلَى ٱلْأَرْضِ مِنَ ٱلْكَٰفِرِينَ دَيَّارًا
markers.english_text: And Noah prayed: "O my Sustainer! Leave not on earth any of those who deny the truth:

markers.arabic_text: إِنَّكَ إِن تَذَرْهُمْ يُضِلُّوا۟ عِبَادَكَ وَلَا يَلِدُوٓا۟ إِلَّا فَاجِرًۭا كَفَّارًۭا
markers.english_text: for, behold, If Thou dost leave them, they will [always try to] lead astray those who worship Thee, and will give birth to nothing but wickedness and stubborn ingratitude.

markers.arabic_text: رَّبِّ ٱغْفِرْ لِى وَلِوَٰلِدَىَّ وَلِمَن دَخَلَ بَيْتِىَ مُؤْمِنًۭا وَلِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَٱلْمُؤْمِنَٰتِ وَلَا تَزِدِ ٱلظَّٰلِمِينَ إِلَّا تَبَارًۢا
markers.english_text: "O my Sustainer! Grant Thy forgiveness unto me and unto my parents, and unto everyone who enters my house as a believer, and unto all believing men and believing women [of later times]; and grant Thou that the doers of evil shall increasingly meet with destruction!"'

markers.arabic_text: بِسْمِ ٱللَّهِ ٱلرَّحْمَٰنِ ٱلرَّحِيمِ قُلْ أُوحِىَ إِلَىَّ أَنَّهُ ٱسْتَمَعَ نَفَرٌۭ مِّنَ ٱلْجِنِّ فَقَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّا سَمِعْنَا قُرْءَانًا عَجَبًۭا
markers.english_text: SAY: "It has been revealed to me that some of the unseen beings gave ear [to this divine writ], and thereupon said [unto their fellow-beings]: "'Verily, we have heard a wondrous discourse,

markers.arabic_text: يَهْدِىٓ إِلَى ٱلرُّشْدِ فَـَٔامَنَّا بِهِۦ ۖ وَلَن نُّشْرِكَ بِرَبِّنَآ أَحَدًۭا
markers.english_text: guiding towards consciousness of what is right; and so We have come to believe in it. And we shall never ascribe divinity to anyone beside our Sustainer,

markers.arabic_text: وَأَنَّهُۥ تَعَٰلَىٰ جَدُّ رَبِّنَا مَا ٱتَّخَذَ صَٰحِبَةًۭ وَلَا وَلَدًۭا
markers.english_text: for [we know] that sublimely exalted is our Sustainer's majesty: no consort has He ever taken unto Himself, nor a son!

markers.arabic_text: وَأَنَّهُۥ كَانَ يَقُولُ سَفِيهُنَا عَلَى ٱللَّهِ شَطَطًۭا
markers.english_text: "'And [now we know] that the foolish among us were wont to say outrageous things about God,

markers.arabic_text: وَأَنَّا ظَنَنَّآ أَن لَّن تَقُولَ ٱلْإِنسُ وَٱلْجِنُّ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ كَذِبًۭا
markers.english_text: and that [we were mistaken when] we thought that neither man nor [any of] the invisible forces would ever tell a lie about God.

markers.arabic_text: وَأَنَّهُۥ كَانَ رِجَالٌۭ مِّنَ ٱلْإِنسِ يَعُوذُونَ بِرِجَالٍۢ مِّنَ ٱلْجِنِّ فَزَادُوهُمْ رَهَقًۭا
markers.english_text: Yet [it has always happened] that certain kinds of humans would seek refuge with certain kinds of [such] invisible forces: but these only increased their confusion –

markers.arabic_text: وَأَنَّهُمْ ظَنُّوا۟ كَمَا ظَنَنتُمْ أَن لَّن يَبْعَثَ ٱللَّهُ أَحَدًۭا
markers.english_text: so much so that they came to think, as you [once] thought, that God would never [again] send forth anyone [as His apostle].

markers.arabic_text: وَأَنَّا لَمَسْنَا ٱلسَّمَآءَ فَوَجَدْنَٰهَا مُلِئَتْ حَرَسًۭا شَدِيدًۭا وَشُهُبًۭا
markers.english_text: "'And [so it happened] that we reached out towards heaven: but we found it filled with mighty guards and flames,

markers.arabic_text: وَأَنَّا كُنَّا نَقْعُدُ مِنْهَا مَقَٰعِدَ لِلسَّمْعِ ۖ فَمَن يَسْتَمِعِ ٱلْءَانَ يَجِدْ لَهُۥ شِهَابًۭا رَّصَدًۭا
markers.english_text: notwithstanding that we were established in positions [which we had thought well-suited] to listening to [whatever secrets might be in] it: and anyone who now [or ever] tries to listen will [likewise] find a flame lying in wait for him!

markers.arabic_text: وَأَنَّا لَا نَدْرِىٓ أَشَرٌّ أُرِيدَ بِمَن فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ أَمْ أَرَادَ بِهِمْ رَبُّهُمْ رَشَدًۭا
markers.english_text: "'And [now we have become aware] that we [created beings] may not know whether evil fortune is intended for [any of] those who live on earth, or whether it is their Sustainer’s will to endow them with consciousness of what is right:

markers.arabic_text: وَأَنَّا مِنَّا ٱلصَّٰلِحُونَ وَمِنَّا دُونَ ذَٰلِكَ ۖ كُنَّا طَرَآئِقَ قِدَدًۭا
markers.english_text: Just as [we do not know how it happens] that some from among us are righteous, while some of us are [far] below that: we have always followed widely divergent paths.

markers.arabic_text: وَأَنَّا ظَنَنَّآ أَن لَّن نُّعْجِزَ ٱللَّهَ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَلَن نُّعْجِزَهُۥ هَرَبًۭا
markers.english_text: "'And, withal, we have come to know that we can never elude God [while we live] on earth, and that we can never elude Him by escaping [from life].

markers.arabic_text: وَأَنَّا لَمَّا سَمِعْنَا ٱلْهُدَىٰٓ ءَامَنَّا بِهِۦ ۖ فَمَن يُؤْمِنۢ بِرَبِّهِۦ فَلَا يَخَافُ بَخْسًۭا وَلَا رَهَقًۭا
markers.english_text: Hence, as soon as we heard this [call to His] guidance, we came to believe in it: and he who believes in his Sustainer need never have fear of loss or injustice.

markers.arabic_text: وَأَنَّا مِنَّا ٱلْمُسْلِمُونَ وَمِنَّا ٱلْقَٰسِطُونَ ۖ فَمَنْ أَسْلَمَ فَأُو۟لَٰٓئِكَ تَحَرَّوْا۟ رَشَدًۭا
markers.english_text: "'Yet [it is true] that among us are such as have surrendered themselves to God - just as there are among us such as have abandoned themselves to wrongdoing. Now as for those who surrender themselves to Him - it is they that have attained to consciousness of what is right;
but as for those who abandon themselves to wrongdoing - they are indeed but fuel for [the fires of] hell!’"

markers.arabic_text: وَأَلَّوِ ٱسْتَقَٰمُوا۟ عَلَى ٱلطَّرِيقَةِ لَأَسْقَيْنَٰهُم مَّآءً غَدَقًۭا
markers.english_text: [KNOW,] THEN, that if they [who have heard Our call] keep firmly to the [right] path, We shall certainly shower them with blessings abundant,

markers.arabic_text: لِّنَفْتِنَهُمْ فِيهِ ۚ وَمَن يُعْرِضْ عَن ذِكْرِ رَبِّهِۦ يَسْلُكْهُ عَذَابًۭا صَعَدًۭا
markers.english_text: so as to test them by this means: for he who shall turn away from the remembrance of his Sustainer, him will He cause to undergo suffering most grievous.

markers.arabic_text: وَأَنَّ ٱلْمَسَٰجِدَ لِلَّهِ فَلَا تَدْعُوا۟ مَعَ ٱللَّهِ أَحَدًۭا
markers.english_text: And [know] that all worship is due to God [alone]: hence, do not invoke anyone side by side with God!

markers.arabic_text: وَأَنَّهُۥ لَمَّا قَامَ عَبْدُ ٱللَّهِ يَدْعُوهُ كَادُوا۟ يَكُونُونَ عَلَيْهِ لِبَدًۭا
markers.english_text: Yet [thus it is] that whenever a servant of God stands up in prayer to Him, they [who are bent on denying the truth] would gladly overwhelm him with their crowds.

markers.arabic_text: قُلْ إِنَّمَآ أَدْعُوا۟ رَبِّى وَلَآ أُشْرِكُ بِهِۦٓ أَحَدًۭا
markers.english_text: Say: "I invoke my Sustainer alone, for I do not ascribe divinity to anyone beside Him."

markers.arabic_text: قُلْ إِنِّى لَآ أَمْلِكُ لَكُمْ ضَرًّۭا وَلَا رَشَدًۭا
markers.english_text: Say: "Verily, it is not in my power to cause you harm or to endow you with consciousness of what is right."

markers.arabic_text: قُلْ إِنِّى لَن يُجِيرَنِى مِنَ ٱللَّهِ أَحَدٌۭ وَلَنْ أَجِدَ مِن دُونِهِۦ مُلْتَحَدًا
markers.english_text: Say: "Verily, no one could ever protect me from God, nor could I ever find a place to hide from Him

markers.arabic_text: إِلَّا بَلَٰغًۭا مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ وَرِسَٰلَٰتِهِۦ ۚ وَمَن يَعْصِ ٱللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُۥ فَإِنَّ لَهُۥ نَارَ جَهَنَّمَ خَٰلِدِينَ فِيهَآ أَبَدًا
markers.english_text: if I should fail to convey [to the world whatever illumination comes to me] from God and His messages." Now as for him who rebels against God and His Apostle - verily, the fire of hell awaits him, therein to abide beyond the count of time.

markers.arabic_text: حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا رَأَوْا۟ مَا يُوعَدُونَ فَسَيَعْلَمُونَ مَنْ أَضْعَفُ نَاصِرًۭا وَأَقَلُّ عَدَدًۭا
markers.english_text: [Let them, then, wait] until the time when they behold that [doom] of which they were forewarned: for then they will come to understand which [kind of man] is more helpless and counts for less!

markers.arabic_text: قُلْ إِنْ أَدْرِىٓ أَقَرِيبٌۭ مَّا تُوعَدُونَ أَمْ يَجْعَلُ لَهُۥ رَبِّىٓ أَمَدًا
markers.english_text: Say: "I do not know whether that [doom] of which you were forewarned is near, or whether my Sustainer has set for it a distant term."

markers.arabic_text: عَٰلِمُ ٱلْغَيْبِ فَلَا يُظْهِرُ عَلَىٰ غَيْبِهِۦٓ أَحَدًا
markers.english_text: He [alone] knows that which is beyond the reach of a created being's perception, and to none does He disclose aught of the mysteries of His Own unfathomable knowledge,

markers.arabic_text: إِلَّا مَنِ ٱرْتَضَىٰ مِن رَّسُولٍۢ فَإِنَّهُۥ يَسْلُكُ مِنۢ بَيْنِ يَدَيْهِ وَمِنْ خَلْفِهِۦ رَصَدًۭا
markers.english_text: unless it be to an apostle whom He has been pleased to elect [there for]: and then He sends forth [the forces of heaven] to watch over him in whatever lies open before him and in what is beyond his ken –

markers.arabic_text: لِّيَعْلَمَ أَن قَدْ أَبْلَغُوا۟ رِسَٰلَٰتِ رَبِّهِمْ وَأَحَاطَ بِمَا لَدَيْهِمْ وَأَحْصَىٰ كُلَّ شَىْءٍ عَدَدًۢا
markers.english_text: so as to make manifest that it is indeed [but] their Sustainer's messages that these [apostles] deliver: for it is He who encompasses [with His knowledge] all that they have [to say], just as He takes count, one by one, of everything [that exists].

markers.arabic_text: بِسْمِ ٱللَّهِ ٱلرَّحْمَٰنِ ٱلرَّحِيمِ يَٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلْمُزَّمِّلُ
markers.english_text: O THOU enwrapped one!
of one-half thereof - or make it a little less than that,
Behold, We shall bestow upon thee a weighty message –

markers.arabic_text: إِنَّ لَكَ فِى ٱلنَّهَارِ سَبْحًۭا طَوِيلًۭا
markers.english_text: whereas by day a long chain of doings is thy portion.

markers.arabic_text: رَّبُّ ٱلْمَشْرِقِ وَٱلْمَغْرِبِ لَآ إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ فَٱتَّخِذْهُ وَكِيلًۭا
markers.english_text: The Sustainer of the east and the west [is He]: there is no deity save Him: hence, ascribe to Him alone the power to determine thy fate,

markers.arabic_text: وَٱصْبِرْ عَلَىٰ مَا يَقُولُونَ وَٱهْجُرْهُمْ هَجْرًۭا جَمِيلًۭا
markers.english_text: and endure with patience whatever people may say [against thee], and avoid them with a comely avoidance.

markers.arabic_text: وَذَرْنِى وَٱلْمُكَذِّبِينَ أُو۟لِى ٱلنَّعْمَةِ وَمَهِّلْهُمْ قَلِيلًا
markers.english_text: And leave Me alone [to deal] with those who give the lie to the truth - those who enjoy the blessings of life [without any thought of God] - and bear thou with them for a little while:
for, behold, heavy fetters [await them] with Us, and a blazing fire,
and food that chokes, and grievous suffering

markers.arabic_text: يَوْمَ تَرْجُفُ ٱلْأَرْضُ وَٱلْجِبَالُ وَكَانَتِ ٱلْجِبَالُ كَثِيبًۭا مَّهِيلًا
markers.english_text: on the Day when the earth and the mountains will be convulsed and the mountains will [crumble and] become like a sand-dune on the move!

markers.arabic_text: إِنَّآ أَرْسَلْنَآ إِلَيْكُمْ رَسُولًۭا شَٰهِدًا عَلَيْكُمْ كَمَآ أَرْسَلْنَآ إِلَىٰ فِرْعَوْنَ رَسُولًۭا
markers.english_text: BEHOLD, [O men,] We have sent unto you an apostle who shall bear witness to the truth before you, even as We sent an apostle unto Pharaoh:

markers.arabic_text: فَعَصَىٰ فِرْعَوْنُ ٱلرَّسُولَ فَأَخَذْنَٰهُ أَخْذًۭا وَبِيلًۭا
markers.english_text: and Pharaoh rebelled against the apostle, whereupon We took him to task with a crushing grip.

markers.arabic_text: فَكَيْفَ تَتَّقُونَ إِن كَفَرْتُمْ يَوْمًۭا يَجْعَلُ ٱلْوِلْدَٰنَ شِيبًا
markers.english_text: How, then, if you refuse to acknowledge the truth, will you protect yourselves on that Day which shall turn the hair of children grey,

markers.arabic_text: ٱلسَّمَآءُ مُنفَطِرٌۢ بِهِۦ ۚ كَانَ وَعْدُهُۥ مَفْعُولًا
markers.english_text: [the Day] on which the skies shall be rent asunder, [and] His promise [of resurrection] fulfilled?

markers.arabic_text: إِنَّ هَٰذِهِۦ تَذْكِرَةٌۭ ۖ فَمَن شَآءَ ٱتَّخَذَ إِلَىٰ رَبِّهِۦ سَبِيلًا
markers.english_text: This, verily, is a reminder: let him who wills, then set out on a way to his Sustainer!

markers.arabic_text: بِسْمِ ٱللَّهِ ٱلرَّحْمَٰنِ ٱلرَّحِيمِ يَٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلْمُدَّثِّرُ
markers.english_text: O THOU [in thy solitude] enfolded!
And thine inner self purify!
And do not through giving seek thyself to gain,
that very Day shall be a day of anguish,
LEAVE Me alone [to deal] with him whom I have created alone,
and children as [love's] witnesses,
and to whose life I gave so wide a scope:
Nay, verily, it is against Our messages that he knowingly, stubbornly sets himself
Behold, [when Our messages are conveyed to one who is bent on denying the truth,] he reflects and meditates [as to how to disprove them] –
and then he looks [around for new arguments],

markers.arabic_text: فَقَالَ إِنْ هَٰذَآ إِلَّا سِحْرٌۭ يُؤْثَرُ
markers.english_text: and says, "All this is mere spellbinding eloquence handed down [from olden times]!
[Hence,] I shall cause him to endure hell-fire [in the life to come]!
making [all truth] visible to mortal man.
NAY, but consider the moon!
Verily, that [hell-fire) is Indeed one of the great [forewarnings] –
[On the Day of Judgment,] every human being will be held in pledge for whatever [evil] he has wrought –
save those who shall have attained to righteousness:
[dwelling] In gardens [of paradise], they will inquire
"What has brought you into hell-fire?"
and neither did we feed the needy;
and we were wont to indulge in sinning together with all [the others] who indulged in it;
and the Day of Judgment we were wont to call a lie –
And so, of no benefit to them could be the intercession of any that would intercede for them.
WHAT, THEN, is amiss with them that they turn away from all admonition
as though they were terrified asses
fleeing from a lion?

markers.arabic_text: بَلْ يُرِيدُ كُلُّ ٱمْرِئٍۢ مِّنْهُمْ أَن يُؤْتَىٰ صُحُفًۭا مُّنَشَّرَةًۭ
markers.english_text: Yea, everyone of them claims that he [himself] ought to have been given revelations unfolded!
Nay, but they do not [believe in and, hence, do not] fear the life to come.
and whoever wills may take it to heart.

markers.arabic_text: بِسْمِ ٱللَّهِ ٱلرَّحْمَٰنِ ٱلرَّحِيمِ لَآ أُقْسِمُ بِيَوْمِ ٱلْقِيَٰمَةِ
markers.english_text: NAY! I call to witness the Day of Resurrection!
But nay! I call to witness the accusing voice of man's own conscience!

markers.arabic_text: بَلَىٰ قَٰدِرِينَ عَلَىٰٓ أَن نُّسَوِّىَ بَنَانَهُۥ
markers.english_text: Yea indeed, We are able to make whole his very finger-tips!
asking [derisively], "When is that Resurrection Day to be?"
and the moon is darkened,
and the sun and the moon are brought together
But nay: no refuge [for thee, O man]!

markers.arabic_text: يُنَبَّؤُا۟ ٱلْإِنسَٰنُ يَوْمَئِذٍۭ بِمَا قَدَّمَ وَأَخَّرَ
markers.english_text: Man will be apprised, on that Day, of what he has done and what he has left undone:
even though he may veil himself in excuses.

markers.arabic_text: لَا تُحَرِّكْ بِهِۦ لِسَانَكَ لِتَعْجَلَ بِهِۦٓ
markers.english_text: MOVE NOT thy tongue in haste, [repeating the words of the revelation:]
for, behold, it is for Us to gather it [in thy heart,] and to cause it to be read [as it ought to be read].
and then, behold, it will be for Us to make its meaning clear.
Some faces will on that Day be bright with happiness,
looking up to their Sustainer;
and some faces will on that Day be overcast with despair,
NAY, but when [the last breath] comes up to the throat [of a dying man],
the while he [himself] knows that this is the parting,
and is enwrapped in the pangs of death
at that time towards thy Sustainer does he feel impelled to turn!
but, on the contrary, he gave the lie to the truth and turned away [from it],
[And yet, O man, thine end comes hourly] nearer unto thee, and nearer –

markers.arabic_text: أَلَمْ يَكُ نُطْفَةًۭ مِّن مَّنِىٍّۢ يُمْنَىٰ
markers.english_text: Was he not once a [mere] drop of sperm that had been spilt,
and thereafter became a germ-cell - whereupon He created and formed [it] in accordance with what [it] was meant to be,
and fashioned out of it the two sexes, the male and the female?

markers.arabic_text: أَلَيْسَ ذَٰلِكَ بِقَٰدِرٍ عَلَىٰٓ أَن يُحْۦِىَ ٱلْمَوْتَىٰ
markers.english_text: Is not He, then; able to bring the dead back to life?

markers.arabic_text: بِسْمِ ٱللَّهِ ٱلرَّحْمَٰنِ ٱلرَّحِيمِ هَلْ أَتَىٰ عَلَى ٱلْإِنسَٰنِ حِينٌۭ مِّنَ ٱلدَّهْرِ لَمْ يَكُن شَيْـًۭٔا مَّذْكُورًا
markers.english_text: HAS THERE [not] been an endless span of time before man [appeared - a time] when he was not yet a thing to be thought of?

markers.arabic_text: إِنَّا خَلَقْنَا ٱلْإِنسَٰنَ مِن نُّطْفَةٍ أَمْشَاجٍۢ نَّبْتَلِيهِ فَجَعَلْنَٰهُ سَمِيعًۢا بَصِيرًا
markers.english_text: Verily, it is We who have created man out of a drop of sperm intermingled, so that We might try him [in his later life]: and therefore We made him a being endowed with hearing and sight.

markers.arabic_text: إِنَّا هَدَيْنَٰهُ ٱلسَّبِيلَ إِمَّا شَاكِرًۭا وَإِمَّا كَفُورًا
markers.english_text: Verily, We have shown him the way: [and it rests with him to prove himself] either grateful or ungrateful.

markers.arabic_text: إِنَّ ٱلْأَبْرَارَ يَشْرَبُونَ مِن كَأْسٍۢ كَانَ مِزَاجُهَا كَافُورًا
markers.english_text: [whereas,] behold, the truly virtuous shall drink from a cup flavoured with the calyx of sweet-smelling flowers:

markers.arabic_text: عَيْنًۭا يَشْرَبُ بِهَا عِبَادُ ٱللَّهِ يُفَجِّرُونَهَا تَفْجِيرًۭا
markers.english_text: a source [of bliss] whereof God's servants shall drink, seeing it flow in a flow abundant.

markers.arabic_text: يُوفُونَ بِٱلنَّذْرِ وَيَخَافُونَ يَوْمًۭا كَانَ شَرُّهُۥ مُسْتَطِيرًۭا
markers.english_text: [The truly virtuous are] they [who] fulfil their vows, and stand in awe of a Day the woe of which is bound to spread far and wide,

markers.arabic_text: وَيُطْعِمُونَ ٱلطَّعَامَ عَلَىٰ حُبِّهِۦ مِسْكِينًۭا وَيَتِيمًۭا وَأَسِيرًا
markers.english_text: and who give food - however great be their own want of it - unto the needy, and the orphan, and the captive,

markers.arabic_text: إِنَّمَا نُطْعِمُكُمْ لِوَجْهِ ٱللَّهِ لَا نُرِيدُ مِنكُمْ جَزَآءًۭ وَلَا شُكُورًا
markers.english_text: [saying, in their hearts,] "We feed you for the sake of God alone: we desire no recompense from you, nor thanks:

markers.arabic_text: إِنَّا نَخَافُ مِن رَّبِّنَا يَوْمًا عَبُوسًۭا قَمْطَرِيرًۭا
markers.english_text: behold, we stand in awe of our Sustainer's judgment on a distressful, fateful Day!"

markers.arabic_text: فَوَقَىٰهُمُ ٱللَّهُ شَرَّ ذَٰلِكَ ٱلْيَوْمِ وَلَقَّىٰهُمْ نَضْرَةًۭ وَسُرُورًۭا
markers.english_text: And so, God will preserve them from the woes of that Day, and will bestow on them brightness and joy,
and will reward them for all their patience in adversity with a garden [of bliss] and with [garments of] silk.

markers.arabic_text: مُّتَّكِـِٔينَ فِيهَا عَلَى ٱلْأَرَآئِكِ ۖ لَا يَرَوْنَ فِيهَا شَمْسًۭا وَلَا زَمْهَرِيرًۭا
markers.english_text: In that [garden] they will on couches recline, and will know therein neither [burning] sun nor cold severe,

markers.arabic_text: وَدَانِيَةً عَلَيْهِمْ ظِلَٰلُهَا وَذُلِّلَتْ قُطُوفُهَا تَذْلِيلًۭا
markers.english_text: since its [blissful] shades will come down low over them, and low will hang down its clusters of fruit, most easy to reach.

markers.arabic_text: وَيُطَافُ عَلَيْهِم بِـَٔانِيَةٍۢ مِّن فِضَّةٍۢ وَأَكْوَابٍۢ كَانَتْ قَوَارِيرَا۠
markers.english_text: And they will be waited upon with vessels of silver and goblets that will [seem to] be crystal –

markers.arabic_text: وَيُسْقَوْنَ فِيهَا كَأْسًۭا كَانَ مِزَاجُهَا زَنجَبِيلًا
markers.english_text: And in that [paradise] they will be given to drink of a cup flavoured with ginger,
[derived from] a source [to be found] therein, whose name is "Seek Thy Way".

markers.arabic_text: ۞ وَيَطُوفُ عَلَيْهِمْ وِلْدَٰنٌۭ مُّخَلَّدُونَ إِذَا رَأَيْتَهُمْ حَسِبْتَهُمْ لُؤْلُؤًۭا مَّنثُورًۭا
markers.english_text: And immortal youths will wait upon them: when thou seest them, thou wouldst deem them to be scattered pearls;

markers.arabic_text: عَٰلِيَهُمْ ثِيَابُ سُندُسٍ خُضْرٌۭ وَإِسْتَبْرَقٌۭ ۖ وَحُلُّوٓا۟ أَسَاوِرَ مِن فِضَّةٍۢ وَسَقَىٰهُمْ رَبُّهُمْ شَرَابًۭا طَهُورًا
markers.english_text: Upon those [blest] will be garments of green silk and brocade; and they will be adorned with bracelets of silver. And their Sustainer will them to drink of a drink most pure.

markers.arabic_text: إِنَّ هَٰذَا كَانَ لَكُمْ جَزَآءًۭ وَكَانَ سَعْيُكُم مَّشْكُورًا
markers.english_text: [And they will be told:] "Verily, all this is your reward since Your endeavour [in life] has met [God's] goodly acceptance!"

markers.arabic_text: إِنَّا نَحْنُ نَزَّلْنَا عَلَيْكَ ٱلْقُرْءَانَ تَنزِيلًۭا
markers.english_text: VERILY, [O believer,] it is We who have bestowed from on high this Qur’an upon thee, step by step - truly a bestowal from on high!

markers.arabic_text: فَٱصْبِرْ لِحُكْمِ رَبِّكَ وَلَا تُطِعْ مِنْهُمْ ءَاثِمًا أَوْ كَفُورًۭا
markers.english_text: Await, then, in all patience thy Sustainer's judgment, and pay no heed to any of them, who is a wilful sinner or an ingrate;
and bear in mind thy Sustainer's name at morn and evening

markers.arabic_text: وَمِنَ ٱلَّيْلِ فَٱسْجُدْ لَهُۥ وَسَبِّحْهُ لَيْلًۭا طَوِيلًا
markers.english_text: and during some of the night, and prostrate thyself before Him, and extol His limitless glory throughout the long night.

markers.arabic_text: إِنَّ هَٰٓؤُلَآءِ يُحِبُّونَ ٱلْعَاجِلَةَ وَيَذَرُونَ وَرَآءَهُمْ يَوْمًۭا ثَقِيلًۭا
markers.english_text: Behold, they [who are unmindful of God] love this fleeting life, and leave behind them [all thought of] a grief-laden Day.

markers.arabic_text: نَّحْنُ خَلَقْنَٰهُمْ وَشَدَدْنَآ أَسْرَهُمْ ۖ وَإِذَا شِئْنَا بَدَّلْنَآ أَمْثَٰلَهُمْ تَبْدِيلًا
markers.english_text: [They will not admit to themselves that] it We who have created them and strengthened their make - and [that] if it be Our will We can replace them entirely with others of their kind.

markers.arabic_text: إِنَّ هَٰذِهِۦ تَذْكِرَةٌۭ ۖ فَمَن شَآءَ ٱتَّخَذَ إِلَىٰ رَبِّهِۦ سَبِيلًۭا
markers.english_text: VERILY, all this is an admonition: whoever, then, so wills, may unto his Sustainer find a way.

markers.arabic_text: وَمَا تَشَآءُونَ إِلَّآ أَن يَشَآءَ ٱللَّهُ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ كَانَ عَلِيمًا حَكِيمًۭا
markers.english_text: But you cannot will it unless God wills [to show you that way]: for, behold, God is indeed all-seeing, wise.

markers.arabic_text: يُدْخِلُ مَن يَشَآءُ فِى رَحْمَتِهِۦ ۚ وَٱلظَّٰلِمِينَ أَعَدَّ لَهُمْ عَذَابًا أَلِيمًۢا
markers.english_text: He admits unto His grace everyone who wills [to be admitted]; but as for the evildoers - for them has He readied grievous suffering [in the life to come].

markers.arabic_text: بِسْمِ ٱللَّهِ ٱلرَّحْمَٰنِ ٱلرَّحِيمِ وَٱلْمُرْسَلَٰتِ عُرْفًۭا
markers.english_text: CONSIDER these [messages,] sent forth in waves
and then storming on with a tempest's force!
thus separating [right and wrong] with all clarity,
and then giving forth a reminder,
[promising] freedom from blame or [offering] a warning!
BEHOLD, all that you are told to expect will surely come to pass.
Thus, [it will come to pass] when the stars are effaced,
and when the sky is rent asunder,
and when the mountains are scattered like dust,
and when all the apostles are called together at a time appointed...
For what day has the term [of all this] been set?
For the Day of Distinction [between the true and the false]!
And what could make thee conceive what that Day of Distinction will be?
Woe on that Day unto those who give the lie to the truth!
Did We not destroy [so many of] those [sinners] of olden days?
And We shall let them be followed by those of later times:
[for] thus do We deal with such as are lost in sin.
Woe on that Day unto those who give the lie to the truth!
Did We not create you out of a humble fluid
which We then let remain in [the womb's] firm keeping
for a term pre-ordained?
Thus have We determined [the nature of man's creation]: and excellent indeed is Our power to determine [what is to be]!
Woe, on that Day unto those who give the lie to the truth!
Have We not caused the earth to hold within itself
the living and the dead?

markers.arabic_text: وَجَعَلْنَا فِيهَا رَوَٰسِىَ شَٰمِخَٰتٍۢ وَأَسْقَيْنَٰكُم مَّآءًۭ فُرَاتًۭا
markers.english_text: and have We not set on it proud, firm mountains, and given you sweet water to drink?
Woe on that Day unto those who give the lie to the truth!

markers.arabic_text: ٱنطَلِقُوٓا۟ إِلَىٰ مَا كُنتُم بِهِۦ تُكَذِّبُونَ
markers.english_text: GO ON towards that [resurrection] which you were wont to call a lie!

markers.arabic_text: ٱنطَلِقُوٓا۟ إِلَىٰ ظِلٍّۢ ذِى ثَلَٰثِ شُعَبٍۢ
markers.english_text: Go on towards the threefold shadow

markers.arabic_text: لَّا ظَلِيلٍۢ وَلَا يُغْنِى مِنَ ٱللَّهَبِ
markers.english_text: that will offer no [cooling] shade and will be of no avail against the flame
which – behold!- will throw up sparks like [burning] logs,
like giant fiery ropes!
Woe on that Day unto those who give the lie to the truth –
that Day on which they will not [be able to] utter a word,
nor be allowed to proffer excuses!
Woe on that Day unto those who give the lie to the truth –
that Day of Distinction [between the true and the false, when they will be told]: "We have brought you together with those [sinners] of olden times;
and if you [think that you] have a subterfuge left, try to outwit Me!"
Woe on that Day unto those who give the lie to the truth!
[AS AGAINST this,] behold, the God-conscious shall dwell amidst [cooling] shades and springs,
and [partake of] whatever fruit they may desire;
Thus, behold, do We reward the doers of good;
EAT [your fill] and enjoy your life for a little while, O you who are lost in sin!

markers.arabic_text: وَإِذَا قِيلَ لَهُمُ ٱرْكَعُوا۟ لَا يَرْكَعُونَ
markers.english_text: and when they are told, "Bow down [before God]", do not bow down:
In what other tiding, then, will they, after this, believe?

markers.arabic_text: بِسْمِ ٱللَّهِ ٱلرَّحْمَٰنِ ٱلرَّحِيمِ عَمَّ يَتَسَآءَلُونَ
markers.english_text: ABOUT WHAT do they [most often] ask one another?
About the awesome tiding [of resurrection],
on which they [so utterly] disagree.
Nay, but in time they will come to understand [it]!
And once again: Nay, but in time they will come to understand!
HAVE WE NOT made the earth a resting-place [for you],
and the mountains [its] pegs?
and We have made your sleep [a symbol of] death
and made the night [its] cloak
and made the day [a symbol of] life.
And We have built above you seven firmaments,
and have placed [therein the sun,] a lamp full of blazing splendour.
And from the wind-driven clouds We send down waters pouring in abundance,
so that We might bring forth thereby grain, and herbs,
and gardens dense with follage.
VERILY, the Day of Distinction [between the true and the false] has indeed its appointed time:

markers.arabic_text: يَوْمَ يُنفَخُ فِى ٱلصُّورِ فَتَأْتُونَ أَفْوَاجًۭا
markers.english_text: the Day when the trumpet [of resurrection] is sounded and you all come forward in multitudes;
and when the skies are opened and become [as wide-flung] gates;
and when the mountains are made to vanish as if they had been a mirage.
[On that Day,] verily, hell will lie in wait [for those who deny the truth] –
a goal for all who are wont to transgress the bounds of what is right!
In it shall they remain for a long time.

markers.arabic_text: لَّا يَذُوقُونَ فِيهَا بَرْدًۭا وَلَا شَرَابًا
markers.english_text: Neither coolness shall they taste therein nor any [thirst-quenching] drink –
only burning despair and ice-cold darkness:
a meet requital [for their sins]!
Behold, they were not expecting to be called to account,
having given the lie to Our messages one and all:
but We have placed on record every single thing [of what they did].
[And so We shall say:] "Taste, then, [the fruit of your evil doings,] for now We shall bestow on you nothing but more and more suffering!"
[But,] verily for the God-conscious there is supreme fulfilment in store:
and a cup [of happiness] overflowing.

markers.arabic_text: لَّا يَسْمَعُونَ فِيهَا لَغْوًۭا وَلَا كِذَّٰبًۭا
markers.english_text: No empty talk will they hear in that [paradise], nor any lie.
[All this will be] a reward from thy Sustainer, a gift in accordance with [His Own] reckoning –

markers.arabic_text: رَّبِّ ٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ وَمَا بَيْنَهُمَا ٱلرَّحْمَٰنِ ۖ لَا يَمْلِكُونَ مِنْهُ خِطَابًۭا
markers.english_text: [a reward from] the Sustainer of the heavens and the earth and all that is between them, the Most Gracious! [And] none shall have it in their power to raise their voices unto Him

markers.arabic_text: يَوْمَ يَقُومُ ٱلرُّوحُ وَٱلْمَلَٰٓئِكَةُ صَفًّۭا ۖ لَّا يَتَكَلَّمُونَ إِلَّا مَنْ أَذِنَ لَهُ ٱلرَّحْمَٰنُ وَقَالَ صَوَابًۭا
markers.english_text: on the Day when all [human] souls and all the angels will stand up in ranks: none will speak but he to whom the Most Gracious will have given leave; and [everyone] will say [only] what is right.

markers.arabic_text: ذَٰلِكَ ٱلْيَوْمُ ٱلْحَقُّ ۖ فَمَن شَآءَ ٱتَّخَذَ إِلَىٰ رَبِّهِۦ مَـَٔابًا
markers.english_text: That will be the Day of Ultimate Truth: whoever wills, then, let him take the path that leads towards his Sustainer!

markers.arabic_text: إِنَّآ أَنذَرْنَٰكُمْ عَذَابًۭا قَرِيبًۭا يَوْمَ يَنظُرُ ٱلْمَرْءُ مَا قَدَّمَتْ يَدَاهُ وَيَقُولُ ٱلْكَافِرُ يَٰلَيْتَنِى كُنتُ تُرَٰبًۢا
markers.english_text: Verily, We have warned you of suffering near at hand - [suffering] on the Day when man shall [clearly] see what his hands have sent ahead, and when he who has denied the truth shall say, "Oh, would that I were mere dust...!"

markers.arabic_text: بِسْمِ ٱللَّهِ ٱلرَّحْمَٰنِ ٱلرَّحِيمِ وَٱلنَّٰزِعَٰتِ غَرْقًۭا
markers.english_text: CONSIDER those [stars] that rise only to set,
and move [in their orbits] with steady motion,
and float [through space] with floating serene,
and thus they fulfil the [Creator's] behest!
[HENCE, think of] the Day when a violent convulsion will convulse [the world],
to be followed by further [convulsions]!
On that Day will [men's] hearts be throbbing,
[And yet,] some say, "What! Are we indeed to be restored to our former state –
even though we may have become [a heap of] crumbling bones?"
[And] they add, "That, then, would be a return with loss!"
[But,] then, that [Last Hour] will be [upon them of a sudden, as if it were] but a single accusing cry -
and then, lo, they will be fully awakened [to the truth]!
HAS THE STORY of Moses ever come within thy ken.

markers.arabic_text: إِذْ نَادَىٰهُ رَبُّهُۥ بِٱلْوَادِ ٱلْمُقَدَّسِ طُوًى
markers.english_text: Lo! His Sustainer called out to him in the twice-hallowed valley:
"Go unto Pharaoh - for, verily, he has transgressed all bounds of what is right -

markers.arabic_text: فَقُلْ هَل لَّكَ إِلَىٰٓ أَن تَزَكَّىٰ
markers.english_text: and say [unto him], 'Art thou desirous of attaining to purity?
[If so,] then I shall guide thee towards [a cognition of] thy Sustainer, so that [henceforth] thou wilt stand in awe [of Him].’"
But [Pharaoh] gave him the lie and rebelliously rejected [all guidance],
and brusquely turned his back [on Moses];
and then he gathered [his great ones], and called [unto his people],
and said, "I am your Lord All-Highest!"
And thereupon God took him to task, [and made him] a warning example in the life to come as well as in this world.

markers.arabic_text: إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَعِبْرَةًۭ لِّمَن يَخْشَىٰٓ
markers.english_text: In this, behold, there is a lesson indeed for all who stand in awe [of God].

markers.arabic_text: ءَأَنتُمْ أَشَدُّ خَلْقًا أَمِ ٱلسَّمَآءُ ۚ بَنَىٰهَا
markers.english_text: [O MEN!] Are you more difficult to create than the heaven which He has built?
High has He reared its vault and formed it in accordance with what it was meant to be;
and He has made dark its night and brought forth its light of day.
And after that, the earth: wide has He spread its expanse,
and has caused its waters to come out of it, and its pastures,
and has made the mountains firm:
[all this] as a means of livelihood for you and your animals.
AND SO, when the great overwhelming event [of, resurrection] comes to pass –
on that Day man will [clearly] remember all that he has ever wrought;
and the blazing fire [of hell] will be lad open before all who [are destined to] see it.
For, unto him who shall have transgressed the bounds of what is right,
and preferred the life of this world [to the good of his soul],
that blazing fire will truly be the goal!

markers.arabic_text: وَأَمَّا مَنْ خَافَ مَقَامَ رَبِّهِۦ وَنَهَى ٱلنَّفْسَ عَنِ ٱلْهَوَىٰ
markers.english_text: But unto him who shall have stood in fear of his Sustainer's Presence, and held back his inner self from base desires,
paradise will truly be the goal!
THEY WILL ASK thee [O Prophet] about the Last Hour: "When will it come to pass?"
[But] how couldst thou tell anything about it,
[seeing that] with thy Sustainer alone rests the beginning and the end [of all knowledge] thereof?
Thou art but [sent] to warn those who stand in awe of it.

markers.arabic_text: كَأَنَّهُمْ يَوْمَ يَرَوْنَهَا لَمْ يَلْبَثُوٓا۟ إِلَّا عَشِيَّةً أَوْ ضُحَىٰهَا
markers.english_text: On the Day when they behold it, [it will seem to them] as if they had tarried [in this world] no longer than one evening or [one night, ending with] its morn!

markers.arabic_text: بِسْمِ ٱللَّهِ ٱلرَّحْمَٰنِ ٱلرَّحِيمِ عَبَسَ وَتَوَلَّىٰٓ
markers.english_text: HE FROWNED and turned away
because the blind man approached him!
Yet for all thou didst know, [O Muhammad,] he might perhaps have grown in purity,
or have been reminded [of the truth], and helped by this reminder.
Now as for him who believes himself to be self-sufficient
to him didst thou give thy whole attention,
although thou art not accountable for his failure to attain to purity;
but as for him who came unto thee full of eagerness
and in awe [of God]
him didst thou disregard!
NAY, VERILY, these [messages] are but a reminder:
and so, whoever is willing may remember Him
in [the light of His] revelations blest with dignity,
lofty and pure,
[borne] by the hands of messengers
noble and most virtuous.
[But only too often] man destroys himself: how stubbornly does he deny the truth!
[Does man ever consider] out of what substance [God] creates him?
Out of a drop of sperm He creates him, and thereupon determines his nature,
and then makes it easy for him to go through life;
and in the end He causes him to die and brings him to the grave;
and then, if it be His will, He shall raise him again to life,
Nay, but [man] has never yet fulfilled what He has enjoined upon him!
Let man, then, consider [the sources of] his food:
[how it is] that We pour down water, pouring it down abundantly;
and then We cleave the earth [with new growth], cleaving it asunder,
and thereupon We cause grain to grow out of it,
and olive trees and date-palms,
and gardens dense with foliage,
and fruits and herbage,
and from his mother and father,
and from his spouse and his children:

markers.arabic_text: لِكُلِّ ٱمْرِئٍۢ مِّنْهُمْ يَوْمَئِذٍۢ شَأْنٌۭ يُغْنِيهِ
markers.english_text: on that Day, to every one of them will his own state be of sufficient concern.
Some faces will on that Day be bright with happiness,
laughing, rejoicing at glad tidings.
And some faces will on that Day with dust be covered,
with darkness overspread:
these, these will be the ones who denied the truth and were immersed in iniquity!
